coupling.note: Delete IPC handler adds surface but is necessary. 3 files touched (dialog-edit-agent.tsx, dialog-manage-agents.tsx, ipc.ts). Main coupling risk is ipc.ts contention with lane-1 (cross-cutting-ipc-reliability) and lane-2 (mcp-management-crash-fixes).

debuggability.note: try/catch on handleSave is good. BUT: no specification that handleSave catch must log the full error (console.error with error object) and show i18n error toast. Also no loading state for initial agent load — silent loading failure.

convergence.note: Save race fix via debounce/queue is convergent. Delete IPC handler follows existing pattern. Validates the electron-store persistence path already established.

surface_area.note: New delete-desktop-custom-agent IPC handler expands preload bridge type in app.tsx. Backward-compatible (optional method). No public type changes. Temperature/top_p validation is internal.

testability.note: Scope does NOT call out extraction of validation/clamping into pure function for unit testing. No test framework exists yet. Delete IPC handler cannot be tested without Electron. Save queue is async effect-heavy.

error_clarity.note: No specification to i18n error messages ('Failed to save custom agents', 'created', 'saved', 'deleted'). Current saveCustomAgents error toast is hardcoded English. handleSave catch not specified at all.

reversibility.note: Each fix is independently revertible. Delete handler is the most coupled (3 files). Locale parsing + validation + try/catch are 1-file changes. Save queue is 1-file change.

findings.summary: No i18n specification for user-facing error messages
findings.evidence: dialog-manage-agents.tsx:97 — 'Failed to save custom agents — changes not persisted' is hardcoded English. dialog-manage-agents.tsx:107 — `"${agent.name}" created` is hardcoded template. dialog-manage-agents.tsx:122 — `"${updated.name}" saved` is hardcoded. dialog-manage-agents.tsx:139 — `"${agent.name}" deleted` is hardcoded. The prefab prompt doesn't mention i18n-ifying these strings.
findings.repair: Add language.t() calls for all error/success toast description strings. Add new i18n keys under dialog.agents.errors.* and dialog.agents.success.* in i18n/en.ts.

findings.summary: Server-side temperature/top_p validation missing from plan scope
findings.evidence: ipc.ts:58-61 — isValidAgentDef only checks typeof obj.id === 'string' && typeof obj.name === 'string' && typeof obj.prompt === 'string'. temperature and top_p are not validated. This allows corrupted data (NaN, Infinity, strings) to persist from direct store writes or IPC replay attacks.
findings.repair: Extend isValidAgentDef to validate temperature and top_p types and ranges: if (obj.temperature !== undefined && (typeof obj.temperature !== 'number' || obj.temperature < 0 || obj.temperature > 2)) return false; same for top_p [0,1].

findings.summary: No extraction of parse/validate logic into testable pure functions
findings.evidence: dialog-edit-agent.tsx:69-70 — temperature/top_p parsing inline in handleSave. Not extractable as unit test. The plan should create parseTemperature(value: string): number | undefined and parseTopP(value: string): number | undefined pure functions.
findings.repair: Extract temperature and top_p parsing into exported pure functions: export function parseTemperature(value: string): number | undefined { ... } and export function parseTopP(value: string): number | undefined { ... }. Place in a shared utility file or at top of dialog-edit-agent.tsx.

findings.summary: Debounce is incorrect fix for save race — need functional updater rollback
findings.evidence: dialog-manage-agents.tsx:81-91 — const prev = customAgents() captures state at call time. A 300ms debounce reduces race window but doesn't eliminate it. Two saves within 300ms still race. Correct fix: setCustomAgents(p => p === agents ? prev : p) in catch block so rollback only reverts if state hasn't changed.
findings.repair: Replace debounce approach with functional updater: try { ... } catch (err) { setCustomAgents(prev => prev === agents ? customAgents() : prev) }. This is atomic — never reverts a concurrent save.

findings.summary: No try/catch error logging specification for handleSave
findings.evidence: dialog-edit-agent.tsx:59-76 — handleSave has zero error handling. If crypto.randomUUID(), .toString(), or props.onSave() throws (e.g., from corrupted agent data), the uncaught exception crashes the Electron renderer with no diagnostic path.
findings.repair: Wrap handleSave body in try/catch. Catch block must: (1) log full error via console.error with agent context, (2) show user-facing error toast via language.t(), (3) NOT close the dialog on error so user can retry.

findings.summary: No loading/error state for initial agent load
findings.evidence: dialog-manage-agents.tsx:41-47 — if (!loaded()) { ... a.getCustomAgents().then(...) } has no loading spinner, no error handling, and no empty setCustomAgents on reject. A failed initial load leaves customAgents as empty array with no diagnostic.
findings.repair: Add loading signal and error signal for initial load. Show spinner during load. Show inline error message on load failure with retry action.

findings.evidence: Prefab prompt suggests Number(value.replace(',', '.')). This fails for other locales that use non-ASCII decimal separators (e.g., Arabic decimal separator '٫', Hindi '।'). Also Number('') → 0, which silently corrupts empty input.
findings.repair: Use targeted Number(value.replace(',', '.')) only for comma-as-decimal locales. Better: validate the raw input client-side (HTML input type=number already normalizes), and only apply comma replacement for pasted text. Guard against empty string before Number().

summary: The prefab architect prompt is sound in scope but has 3 blocking gaps: (1) i18n for error messages not specified, (2) server-side temperature/top_p validation not called out, (3) no extractable pure functions for unit testing. Additionally, the debounce approach for the save race is insufficient — a functional updater rollback is required.
